tossups.question: One of this author’s poems describes “angular / starched consonants” that “braid the tongue at its root” and “foreign cities where the sun's a ghost”. That poem by this author ends by stating, “and once more on our lips we'll taste Greek (+) salt”. Another poem by this author claims that “in silences / things yield and almost betray / their ultimate secrets”, and describes a “little path” that goes through “cane-tufts” and the title (*) plants. This poet wrote, “Laureate poets / seem to wander among plants / no one knows” in a poem from his collection Cuttlefish Bones. For 10 points, name this Italian poet of “The Lemon Trees”.
tossups.question_sanitized: One of this author's poems describes "angular / starched consonants" that "braid the tongue at its root" and "foreign cities where the sun's a ghost". That poem by this author ends by stating, "and once more on our lips we'll taste Greek (+) salt". Another poem by this author claims that "in silences / things yield and almost betray / their ultimate secrets", and describes a "little path" that goes through "cane-tufts" and the title (*) plants. This poet wrote, "Laureate poets / seem to wander among plants / no one knows" in a poem from his collection Cuttlefish Bones. For 10 points, name this Italian poet of "The Lemon Trees".

tossups.question: A poet from this country addressed a creature as a “brief rainbow, iris, twin to the glance mounted within your lashes” and “love’s arrow on earth”. An author from this country described how “sinking is sweet in such a sea” in a 15-line sonnet addressed to infinity. A poet from this country contrasted “laureate poets” who “seem to wander” while “even the poor / know that richness” of the title plants. This country was home to the (*) hermeticist writer of the collection Cuttlefish Bones. The Earl of Surrey translated a work from this country that describes “the sun’s rays turned pale with grief” in a collection about a woman he met on Good Friday. An author from this country addressed “you who hear the sound in scattered rhymes” in a collection dedicated to Laura. For 10 points, name this home country of Eugenio Montale and Petrarch.
tossups.answer: Italy [accept Italia ] (The first line refers to Eugenio Montale’s “The Eel. “The Infinite” is by Giacomo Leopardi.”)
tossups.answer_sanitized: Italy [accept  Italia ] (The first line refers to Eugenio Montale's "The Eel. "The Infinite" is by Giacomo Leopardi.")
tossups.question_sanitized: A poet from this country addressed a creature as a "brief rainbow, iris, twin to the glance mounted within your lashes" and "love's arrow on earth". An author from this country described how "sinking is sweet in such a sea" in a 15-line sonnet addressed to infinity. A poet from this country contrasted "laureate poets" who "seem to wander" while "even the poor / know that richness" of the title plants. This country was home to the (*) hermeticist writer of the collection Cuttlefish Bones. The Earl of Surrey translated a work from this country that describes "the sun's rays turned pale with grief" in a collection about a woman he met on Good Friday. An author from this country addressed "you who hear the sound in scattered rhymes" in a collection dedicated to Laura. For 10 points, name this home country of Eugenio Montale and Petrarch.

tossups.question: A poem by this author describes the Good as “the statue in the somnolence / of noon, and the cloud, and the lofty hawk.” Another poem by this author rebukes a word that “scrutinises / our shapeless soul, and in letters of fire” and ends with the claim “All that we can tell you today is this: / what we aren't, and what we don't wish for.” This poet’s first book includes poems opening “Often have I met the ill of living” and “Don't ask us for the word.” This poet described how “the heart’s ice thaws / and songs pelt / into the breast / and trumpets of gold pour forth / epiphanies of Light” when “one day, through a gate ajar, / among the (*) trees in a courtyard, / we see” the title things. This poet asked “can you not see / that she is your sister?” in “The Eel” and included “Lemon Trees” in the volume Cuttlefish Bones. Salvatore Quasimodo, Giuseppe Ungaretti, and—for 10 points—what other poet founded Hermeticism?
tossups.question_sanitized: A poem by this author describes the Good as "the statue in the somnolence / of noon, and the cloud, and the lofty hawk." Another poem by this author rebukes a word that "scrutinises / our shapeless soul, and in letters of fire" and ends with the claim "All that we can tell you today is this: / what we aren't, and what we don't wish for." This poet's first book includes poems opening "Often have I met the ill of living" and "Don't ask us for the word." This poet described how "the heart's ice thaws / and songs pelt / into the breast / and trumpets of gold pour forth / epiphanies of Light" when "one day, through a gate ajar, / among the (*) trees in a courtyard, / we see" the title things. This poet asked "can you not see / that she is your sister?" in "The Eel" and included "Lemon Trees" in the volume Cuttlefish Bones. Salvatore Quasimodo, Giuseppe Ungaretti, and-for 10 points-what other poet founded Hermeticism?

bonuses.leadin: This poet asked, "Do you know the land where the lemon trees bloom?" in "Mignon," included in a bildungsroman whose protagonist joins the Tower Society. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this author whose time in Italy inspired his Italian Journey, Venetian Epigrams, and Roman Elegies.

This country's Romantic-era poem "Exile Song" responds to Goethe's question with, "My land has palm trees where the thrush sings." Giuseppe Ungaretti taught Italian at this country's São Paulo University.

This poet wrote that "a glimpse of yellow lemons" causes "the golden trumpets of radiance" to "thunder their songs" in "Lemon Trees," the first poem from the collection Cuttlefish Bones. Unlike Ungaretti and Quasimodo, he denied being a member of Italian Hermeticism.
answers: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe [The novel is Wilhelm Meister's Apprenticeship.]
Brazil [Federative Republic of Brazil; or República Federativa do Brasil]
answers_sanitized: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe [The novel is Wilhelm Meister's Apprenticeship.]
Brazil [Federative Republic of Brazil; or Republica Federativa do Brasil]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This poet asked, "Do you know the land where the lemon trees bloom?" in "Mignon," included in a bildungsroman whose protagonist joins the Tower Society. For 10 points each:

This poet wrote that "a glimpse of yellow lemons" causes "the golden trumpets of radiance" to "thunder their songs" in "Lemon Trees," the first poem from the collection Cuttlefish Bones. Unlike Ungaretti and Quasimodo, he denied being a member of Italian Hermeticism.

bonuses.leadin: In his collection Translations from the Natural World, Les Murray described these “humble” animals as “spacefarers past living planetfall.” For 10 points each:
parts: Identify these animals. In a poem titled for them, the speaker informs the reader that “This, today, is all we can tell you: what we are not, what we do not want.”
This Italian Hermeticist wrote “Cuttlefish Bones,” and admonished his fellow poets for only walking “among plants with rare names” in his poem “The Lemons.”
In a book-length treatise, Arshi Pipa compared Eugenio Montale to this earlier Italian. This poet standardized the Tuscan dialect with his La Vita Nuova, which wasinspired by his love for Beatrice.
answers: cuttlefish [or “Cuttlefish Bones;” accept “Ossi di seppia;” prompt on fish, even though they aren’t really fish]
answers_sanitized: cuttlefish [or "Cuttlefish Bones;" accept "Ossi di seppia;" prompt on fish, even though they aren't really fish]

In a book-length treatise, Arshi Pipa compared Eugenio Montale to this earlier Italian. This poet standardized the Tuscan dialect with his La Vita Nuova, which wasinspired by his love for Beatrice.

bonuses.leadin: For 10 points each, name these things about the Hermetic movements:
parts: This first of the three great disciplines of Hermeticism was linked by Trismegistus to the workings of the sun. Many Medieval scientists, such as Albertus Magnus and Philippus Paracelsus, considered this discipline to be of great importance.
This Hermetic poet fell in love with Irma Brandeis partially due to their shared interest in Dante. Brandeis inspired the character of Clizia in his collection The Occasions .
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: For 10 points each, name these things about the Hermetic movements:
parts_sanitized: This first of the three great disciplines of Hermeticism was linked by Trismegistus to the workings of the sun. Many Medieval scientists, such as Albertus Magnus and Philippus Paracelsus, considered this discipline to be of great importance.
This Hermetic poet fell in love with Irma Brandeis partially due to their shared interest in Dante. Brandeis inspired the character of Clizia in his collection The Occasions .

bonuses.leadin: During the premiere of this musical, its writer Franz Liebkind ran onstage to confront the audience but was accidentally knocked out despite his Wehrmacht helmet. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this musical within The Producers, an homage to the Nazi party which becomes a success despite the efforts of its producers, Max Bialystock and Leo Bloom, who intended it to flop as part of a scam.

The real Hitler’s last spring was far from enjoyable. After this Waffen SS general refused to attack the 1st Belorussian Front, Hitler went on a tirade on April 22 where he declared that all was lost, as depicted in Downfall..

In The Hitler Spring, this Italian poet described “an eternal angel passed in flight” and engulfed by “an eerie emptiness lit and festooned with swastikas.” He also wrote the collection Cuttlefish Bones.
answers_sanitized: Springtime for Hitler [do not accept The Producers]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: During the premiere of this musical, its writer Franz Liebkind ran onstage to confront the audience but was accidentally knocked out despite his Wehrmacht helmet. For 10 points each:

In The Hitler Spring, this Italian poet described "an eternal angel passed in flight" and engulfed by "an eerie emptiness lit and festooned with swastikas." He also wrote the collection Cuttlefish Bones.

bonuses.leadin: This poet asked “Can you not see she’s your sister?” at the end of a poem describing a “siren of the freezing oceans” entitled “The Eel.” For 10 points each:
parts: Name this winner of the 1975 Nobel Prize in Literature and author of the collection Cuttlefish Bones.
Montale was a forerunner of the Hermeticist movement, which was based in this country. A much earlier poet from this country collected many songs addressed to Laura in his Il Canzoniere.

A Montale poem compares the illusion of several of these objects to “noisy cities where the blue appears only in fragments high up among the towering shapes.” Coleridge wrote a poem about one of these objects after his wife dropped scalding milk on his foot and he could not accompany some visitors on a walk.

A Montale poem compares the illusion of several of these objects to "noisy cities where the blue appears only in fragments high up among the towering shapes." Coleridge wrote a poem about one of these objects after his wife dropped scalding milk on his foot and he could not accompany some visitors on a walk.

bonuses.leadin: In one poem, this poet of "The House of the Customs Officer" described how other authors "walk only among plants / of unfamiliar name" but he prefers to be "among trunks of the lemon trees". For 10 points each:
parts: Identify this Nobel-winning Italian poet of Cuttlefish Bones.
Montale's poem "Arsenio" was translated into English by this American Confessional poet of the collections Lord Weary's Castle and For the Union Dead.

Dora Markus, the title figure of one of Montale's poems, owns a charm of one of these animals made from ivory. Giacomo Leopardi riffed on a Greek work about these creatures and frogs in an account of their war with the crabs.
answers_sanitized: Robert Lowell [or Robert Traill Spence "Cal" Lowell IV]

bonuses.leadin: Characters in this play are forced to put on an absurd comedy because no good ones come from France anymore. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this play that opens with a rehearsal of The Rules of the Game interrupted by a family’s entrance. At the end of this play, actors debate whether a character’s suicide following a girl drowning in a fountain is real or fake.
Six Characters in Search of an Author was written by this Italian author of Right You Are (If You Think You Are) and Tonight We Improvise.
This other Italian Nobel winner was a frequent contributor to the Corriere della Sera, and published poetry collections like The Storm and Cuttlefish Bones.
answers: Six Characters in Search of an Author [or Sei personaggi in cerca d’autore]
